name: Gandia Beach and Safor Greenway Loop
name_original: Gandia — Tour por la playa y el Grau
description: Enjoy an easy 20.1-mile touring cycling route along Gandia Beach and through the lively Grau area, perfect for families.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Gandia feature a diverse landscape, combining coastal plains with challenging mountainous terrain. The region is characterized by greenways, river routes, and wetlands, alongside extensive orange groves and Mediterranean vegetation. Cyclists can explore old railway lines, coastal paths, and ascend to panoramic viewpoints in nearby mountain ranges. Best touring cycling routes around Gandia The most popular touring cycling route is Gandia Beach and Safor Greenway Loop, a 20.1 miles (32.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 59 minutes to complete, winding through coastal areas and orange groves. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Ullal de l’Estany del Duc – Gandia Beach loop from Gandia, an easy 17.3 miles (27.9 km) path. This route offers scenic views along the coast and passes through wetland areas. Local touring cyclists also love the Las Rotas Beach – Greenway of Safor loop from Gandia, a 74.3 miles (119.6 km) trail leading through coastal scenery and the Safor Greenway, often completed in about 7 hours 31 minutes. Touring cycling around Gandia is defined by coastal plains, challenging mountainous terrain, and greenways. The network offers options for various ability levels, from leisurely rides through orange groves to demanding ascents. The routes in Gandia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 315 reviews. More than 4000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Gandia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Gandia?
A: Gandia offers a diverse landscape for touring cyclists, ranging from flat, easy-going coastal plains to challenging mountainous terrain. You'll find routes winding through extensive orange groves, Mediterranean vegetation, and quiet fields, as well as paths along old railway lines and river routes. For more intense rides, nearby mountains like La Safor and Sierra de Mariola Natural Park provide rugged trails and significant ascents.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly touring cycling routes in Gandia?
A: Yes, Gandia has several easy and family-friendly options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e917320513/gandia-beach-and-safor-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gandia Beach and Safor Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 32.4 km trail that is mostly flat and suitable for all fitness levels. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33735068" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ullal de l’Estany del Duc – Gandia Beach loop from Gandia&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 27.9 km path offering scenic coastal views and passing through wetland areas. The Camino Natural Vía Verde de la Safor, part of the Greenway of Safor, is also ideal for relaxed excursions.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Gandia?
A: Gandia benefits from an excellent climate for cycling almost year-round, with over 300 days of sunshine. The best months for touring cycling are typically from &lt;strong&gt;October to May&lt;/strong&gt;, when temperatures are milder and more comfortable for longer rides. Summers can be quite warm, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer opportunities to explore Gandia's natural beauty and historical sites. You can cycle along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/650716" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Greenway of Safor&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy the coastal scenery of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/359882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gandia Beach&lt;/a&gt;, or discover wetland areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5352302" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marjal de la Safor Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5968459" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Duke's Pond Spring&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5152261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salty Spring&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4866511" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural area of the "Font Salà"&lt;/a&gt; are also notable natural monuments. Inland, routes like the Serpis River Route showcase beautiful landscapes and historical elements.

Q: Are there challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking more demanding rides, options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20536401" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Rotas Beach – Greenway of Safor loop from Gandia&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 119.6 km trail with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31550565" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serpis River – Azud del Infierno or del Forn loop from Gandia&lt;/a&gt; also presents a challenging 63.8 km ride. Mountain biking trails in the Sierra de Falconera provide challenging ascents and stunning views, while other hard road rides feature significant elevation gains in the mountains.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Gandia?
A: There are over 120 touring cycling routes available around Gandia, catering to a wide range of abilities. This includes 40 easy routes, 30 moderate routes, and 56 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Gandia?
A: The touring cycling routes in Gandia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 315 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, the mix of coastal and mountainous terrain, and the well-maintained greenways that offer a pleasant cycling experience.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Gandia?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Gandia are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e917320513/gandia-beach-and-safor-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gandia Beach and Safor Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33735068" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ullal de l’Estany del Duc – Gandia Beach loop from Gandia&lt;/a&gt;. These circular routes make planning your ride convenient.

Q: Can I access touring cycling routes using public transport in Gandia?
A: Gandia has a well-developed public transport network, including train and bus services, which can help you reach various starting points for cycling routes. While specific routes may vary, the city's central location and transport links make it feasible to access many areas suitable for touring cycling. It's advisable to check local transport schedules and bicycle carriage policies beforehand.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Gandia pass through or near charming coastal towns and villages where you can find cafes, restaurants, and pubs. The Camino Natural Vía Verde de la Safor, for instance, is known to have picnic areas along the way. Exploring routes that lead to places like Xativa or along the coast will offer numerous opportunities for refreshments.

Q: What are some moderate touring cycling routes around Gandia?
A: For a moderately challenging ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1673042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ullal de l’Estany del Duc – View of Cullera Bay loop from Gandia&lt;/a&gt;. This 67.1 km route offers a good balance of distance and elevation, providing scenic views without being overly strenuous, making it suitable for cyclists with a reasonable level of fitness.
tips: The Vía Verde is a section of lane where cars are not allowed and is for the exclusive use of cyclists or walkers. This route, in perfect condition and with a paved surface, connects the city of Gandía with Oliva, going through very picturesque landscapes. It is all flat and straight so there is no technical difficulty, ideal for a walk with the whole family.

A historical icon of the city of Gandía, this palace used to be the residence of the renowned Borja. The exterior is not flashy and can go unnoticed, but the interior is very picturesque and ostentatious, something usual for the time in this type of palace. It deserves a visit!

A short distance to the north of Gandia, in the direction of Xeraco, we find l'Alqueria del Duc and next to it a splendid natural area worth visiting, both on foot and by bicycle. Totally recommended for the whole family.
In addition to l'Ullal (spring) of l'Estany located on the very side of l'Alqueria, a few meters away we can also enjoy other springs (la Perla, Leocàdia and Fosc) and, a little further away, we can visit the Marjal dels Borrons which is more beautiful than all the other wetlands in the area.
Honestly, one of the most beautiful areas of La Safor.

One of the most beautiful beaches in the province of Valencia. The maritime route makes it even more colorful and picturesque since it can be traveled from end to end either on foot, running or by bike. It is a very nice beach to spend the summer as well as to walk during the rest of the year.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the Gandia — Tour along the beach and the Grau?
faqs.answer: This cycling tour is approximately 32.4 kilometers (20.1 miles) long and typically takes around 2 hours to complete at a relaxed pace. The duration can vary depending on your speed and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Is this cycling tour suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is considered an easy bike ride, making it ideal for beginners, families, and casual cyclists. It features mostly paved surfaces and has minimal elevation gain, ensuring a smooth and comfortable experience for nearly any fitness level.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Gandia — Tour along the beach and the Grau?
faqs.answer: While specific parking locations aren't detailed, Gandia's coastal areas, especially near the beach and Grau, typically offer various parking options. Look for public parking areas near the start of the cycle path along Gandia Beach or in the Grau district.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this route is predominantly flat and features paved surfaces. This makes for a very smooth and accessible ride, perfect for touring bicycles and suitable for all skill levels. The cycle path is well-maintained and runs directly along the beach for much of the way.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque coastal views, riding along one of Valencia's most beautiful beaches with fine golden sand and clear waters. The route also passes through the lively Puerto de Gandia marina and the port, offering glimpses of maritime life and historic warehouses. You'll enjoy continuous views of the Mediterranean Sea.

faqs.question: Are there any notable sights or highlights along the Gandia — Tour along the beach and the Grau?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several points of interest. You'll pass by the Greenway of Safor, ride along Gandia Beach, and get views of the port and marina. While not directly on the route, the historic Ducal Palace of the Borgias in Gandia is nearby and can be explored before or after your ride.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with sections of other official trails. Notably, it covers parts of El Camí IT5TR5 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana and the Via Verda de la Safor, including La via verda de la Safor Gandia - Oliva.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: Given that the route runs along Gandia Beach and through the Grau area, you'll find numerous cafes, restaurants, and shops, especially during peak season. These offer plenty of opportunities to stop for drinks, snacks, or a meal.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The coastal climate of Gandia makes this route enjoyable for much of the year. Spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) offer pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds. Summer can be very warm, but cycling early in the morning or late afternoon can still be comfortable. Winters are generally mild.

faqs.question: Is the Gandia — Tour along the beach and the Grau dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is a public cycling path, regulations regarding dogs on beaches and in specific public areas in Gandia can vary. It's best to check local signage or official Gandia tourism information for the most current rules on bringing dogs, especially regarding leash requirements and access to beach areas.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Gandia — Tour along the beach and the Grau. It is a publicly accessible cycling route.

waypoints.name: Ducal Palace of the Borgias (Palau Ducal dels Borja), Gandia
